new_points.title: Attempted murder arrest after car hits pedestrians in central London
new_points.content: The incident took place in central London in the early hours, leaving a woman in a critical condition.

new_points.title: You Can’t Game Your Way to a Real Education
new_points.content: Technology must return to its proper place in the classroom — as a supplemental tool rather than the source and summit of education.

new_points.title: What are Iran's longterm goals for a peace deal with the U.S.?
new_points.content: NPR's Elissa Nadworny speaks to Narges Bajoghli, associate professor of Middle East studies at Johns Hopkins, about Iran's long-term goals.

new_points.title: Trump says US negotiators will head to Pakistan for Iran ceasefire talks
new_points.content: US President warns of severe repercussions if Iran 'doesn't take the deal'.

new_points.title: Cracks are starting to form on fusion energy’s funding boom
new_points.content: Fusion startups and investors risk deepening fissures if some key disagreements aren't settled amicably.

new_points.title: Gen Z is ‘Chinamaxxing’—and it’s less a love letter to Beijing than an indictment of America
new_points.content: A viral TikTok trend is handing Beijing a cultural moment that decades of propaganda never could. Are we entering the Chinese century?
